title: When will GPT-5.6 be released?
description: Bucketed market on the public release date of OpenAI GPT-5.6.
resolution_rules: Resolves according to the calendar date ET on which OpenAI's GPT-5.6 model is made available to the general public. GPT-5.6 refers to a product explicitly named GPT-5.6, or a variant recognized as a direct successor to GPT-5.5. Qualifying task-specialized, cost-efficiency, and o-series reasoning variants count. Products labeled GPT-6 or similar do not count. A qualifying model must be publicly accessible, including open beta or open rolling waitlist signups; closed beta or private access does not suffice. Primary resolution source is official OpenAI information, with additional verification from consensus credible reporting.
snapshot_note: Verified from public Polymarket page text. Outcome probabilities remain dynamic.

title: GPT-5.6 released by June 30, 2026?
description: Binary outcome in the GPT-5.6 released-by market family.
resolution_rules: Resolves Yes if OpenAI's GPT-5.6 model is made available to the general public by the specified date ET. GPT-5.6 refers to a product explicitly named GPT-5.6, or a variant recognized as a direct successor to GPT-5.5. Qualifying task-specialized, cost-efficiency, and o-series reasoning variants count. Products labeled GPT-6 or similar do not count. A qualifying model must be publicly accessible, including open beta or open rolling waitlist signups; closed beta or private access does not suffice. Primary resolution source is official OpenAI information, with additional verification from consensus credible reporting.
snapshot_note: Verified from public Polymarket search/page text. Exact price varies by crawl and should be refreshed before formal promotion.

title: Claude 5 released by...?
description: Date-bucket market family on whether Anthropic Claude 5 is publicly released by a target date.
resolution_rules: Resolves Yes if Anthropic's Claude 5 model is made available to the general public by the target date. Claude 5 must be launched and publicly accessible, including open beta or open rolling waitlist signups; closed beta or private access does not suffice. Claude 5 refers to a product explicitly named Claude 5 or one recognized as a successor to Claude 4. Products labeled Claude 4.5 or similar do not count. Primary resolution source is official Anthropic information, with additional verification from consensus credible reporting.
snapshot_note: Verified from public Polymarket page text. This is indirect for Claude 4.8 Opus because Claude 4.5 or similar labels do not count.

title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of June?
description: Binary market on whether Strait of Hormuz traffic normalizes by the end of June 2026.
resolution_rules: Resolves Yes if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls, Arrivals of Ships, for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and June 30, 2026. Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch are not considered. Resolution source is IMF Portwatch Strait of Hormuz transit calls data.
snapshot_note: Verified from public Polymarket page text. Objective traffic metric is indirect for proposal acceptance or negotiation status.

title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
description: Date-variant market on whether the US and Iran agree to a permanent peace deal.
resolution_rules: Resolves Yes if the US and Iran agree to a permanent peace deal explicitly ending hostilities, signed or confirmed by both governments, by the target date. Temporary ceasefires or non-definitive progress do not count.
snapshot_note: Public page verified market title, date buckets, prices, and volume. Resolution summary still relies partly on user-provided Grok output; re-open specific outcome page before formal promotion.

title: Amazon 2026 capex above ___?
description: Threshold market family on Amazon 2026 capital expenditures.
resolution_rules: Resolves Yes if Amazon's Purchases of Property and Equipment, capital expenditures, for full-year 2026 as reported in official company earnings materials for Q4 2026 is above the listed amount. Uses the most numerically precise version of the metric in official earnings materials, including press releases, investor presentations, regulatory filings, and earnings webcast materials if needed. Alternate metrics that differ in definition or scope are not considered.
snapshot_note: Verified from public Polymarket page text. Weak proxy for aggregate AI VC/capex/ROI thesis.

title: Will the 30-year Mortgage Rate hit __ in 2026?
description: Threshold market family on US 30-year fixed mortgage rates in 2026.
resolution_rules: Resolves Yes if, for any week ending on or before December 31, 2026, the US 30-year Fixed-Rate Mortgage interest rate is equal to or above the listed rate according to the Freddie Mac weekly Primary Mortgage Market Survey. Resolves as soon as the rate is equal to or greater than the listed price, or once final data for the period has been published. Revisions are not considered.
snapshot_note: Verified from public Polymarket page text. Weak proxy for home-overpricing thesis because it resolves rate thresholds, not valuation.

title: How many Fed rate cuts in 2026?
description: Multi-outcome market on the number of Federal Reserve rate cuts in 2026.
resolution_rules: Counts 25 bps-equivalent reductions in the Federal Reserve target federal funds rate during calendar year 2026, based on official FOMC/Federal Reserve decisions. Emergency cuts qualify if they reduce the federal funds target range during 2026. Outcomes are bucketed by number of cuts.
snapshot_note: Verified from public Polymarket page text. Best indirect market for no-cuts-until-2028 source because it only covers the 2026 portion of the claim.

title: Fed rate cut by...?
description: Meeting-specific market family on whether the Fed cuts rates by a given 2026 FOMC date.
resolution_rules: Resolves Yes if the upper bound of the Federal Reserve target federal funds rate range decreases at or before the specified 2026 FOMC meeting. Emergency cuts qualify if they reduce the upper bound of the target range before the market's specified deadline.
snapshot_note: Verified from public Polymarket page text. Acceptable supporting market for the Fed horizon-mismatch case, not a separate direct expression of no cuts until 2028.
